output_fields.title: string
output_fields.company: string — WARNING: usually the recruiter/headhunter, not the actual employer
output_fields.postedDate: null in most cases — NOT reliable
output_fields.firstSeenAt: ISO datetime — when scraper FIRST found this job (use for cache-based stale detection)
output_fields.lastSeenAt: ISO datetime — last time scraper saw this job
output_fields.expiredAt: ISO datetime — when job was removed from site
output_fields.portalUrl: string — Cadremploi URL to view job
output_fields.description: string — full description, often contains actual company details

stale_detection_note: postedDate is null for most listings. Use cache-based approach: store jobId + firstSeenAt on first scrape, alert when job persists 60+ days in cache (lastSeenAt - firstSeenAt >= 60 days).
do_not: use company field for TAM matching — it shows the recruiter, not the food manufacturer
use keyword DRH industrie alimentaire — returns 0 results
expect datePosted — it is null in ~95% of listings
